text:
Once upon a time in a sun-soaked town, there lived a raccoon named Rusty. Rusty was no ordinary animal; every night, after a full day foraging for nuts, he'd retreat to his secret hideaway—a little rock cave beside a babbling brook.

It was there that Rusty invented the most amazing snuckie. Every snack he took was wrapped in a silk-like moss. They were sparkly! Rusty named them "moonbeams". People from the towns nearby knew he had special treats hidden away.

One sunny day, while collecting moonbeams, Rusty met a curious rabbit named Lily. She seemed a bit lonely. "Hi there," she giggled. "I've never seen moonbeams—they're so bright."

Rusty regaled her with tales of his nightly adventures and the moonbeams. Their friendship started. Every moonlit night, after they giggled over silly stories, Lily would take Rusty's moonbeams
